short_description: Download & Save Brazzpw Videos Easily for Offline Viewing (Browser Extension)
long_description: Brazzpw Downloader is a browser extension that gives you a native workflow for saving videos from supported BrazzPW pages. Instead of hunting through page source or relying on generic copy-paste downloader sites, this extension works directly on the page you are viewing, detecting media candidates as they become available.

faqs.question: How do I download a BrazzPW video?
faqs.answer: Open a supported BrazzPW page, press play if needed, then use the player download button, popup, or right-click menu to review any detected candidates.

faqs.question: What formats can it detect?
faqs.answer: The extension checks video and source tags plus Open Graph and Twitter stream metadata. When the page exposes a usable stream, that may include direct MP4 or HLS-style candidates.

faqs.question: Will it always show multiple quality options?
faqs.answer: Available options depend on what the source page exposes. Not every BrazzPW page reveals multiple variants.

faqs.question: Where are downloads saved?
faqs.answer: Files are saved to an organized BrazzPW download folder in your browser's default download location.

faqs.question: Do I need to press play first?
faqs.answer: Often yes. The media stream may only become visible after playback starts or player scripts run.

faqs.question: Is this extension release-ready?
faqs.answer: This is a released extension. If you encounter issues, report them through GitHub Issues.

faqs.question: Does it use a remote downloader site?
faqs.answer: No. The workflow is in-browser detection plus extension processing. Remote services are used only for authentication and release checks.

permissions.description: Only download content you own or have explicit permission to save

features: Detects media candidates exposed on BrazzPW video and player pages
Adds an in-page download button near the player
Works through the extension popup or right-click context menu

Saves organized MP4 files to a dedicated BrazzPW folder
In-page download button configured around the BrazzPW player wrapper
Detection from video and source tags plus Open Graph and Twitter media metadata
Shared offscreen processing for direct MP4 and HLS-style candidates
Right-click context menu for page and video contexts
Extension popup with detected media candidates
In-page download manager with progress and status
Organized downloads saved to a BrazzPW folder

why: BrazzPW pages often expose the final video stream only after the player initializes and playback begins. This means right-clicking the page or inspecting network requests may not reveal a usable media URL, and generic downloader sites often miss page-specific player behavior.

Brazzpw Downloader is built around the BrazzPW player wrapper and on-page media detection. It watches for video sources that become available during playback and presents them through a simple in-page button, popup, or context menu. This keeps the download workflow on the page itself, without requiring you to copy URLs or use external tools.
how_it_works: Install the extension from the latest release.
Open BrazzPW and go to a supported video page.
Start playback so the extension can detect the media.
Open the popup or use the on-page controls.
Choose the quality option you want.
Start the download and wait for the MP4 export to finish.
tutorial: Install the Brazzpw Downloader extension from the latest GitHub release.
Open your browser and navigate to a BrazzPW video or player page.
Press play on the video player to let the stream initialize.
Look for the download button that appears near the player wrapper.
Alternatively, click the extension icon in your toolbar to open the popup.
Right-click anywhere on the page or on the video itself to use the context menu.
Select the media candidate you want from the available options.
Wait for the download to complete and save the MP4 file.

troubleshooting: The download button does not appear on the page. Press play on the video first — the media stream may only become visible after playback starts.
No media candidates are detected. Try refreshing the page and starting playback again. Some pages require user interaction before exposing the video source.
The download fails or stops midway. Check your internet connection and try again. If the issue persists, the page may not be exposing a complete media stream.
The popup shows no options. Make sure you are on a supported BrazzPW video or player page. Navigate to a specific video and press play before opening the popup.
I see an authentication error. Complete the email verification through the OTP flow. You need to sign in to activate your trial or license.

No credit card required for the trial
Unlimited downloads are available with a paid license
